sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports right elbow pain exacerbated by tennis activity over the past three weeks. He describes the pain as sharp and stabbing, with a pain level of 8/10 during use and 3/10 at rest. He has taken ibuprofen and acetaminophen with limited relief. He denies recent trauma or a popping sensation. He reports a history of playing tennis and enjoying it, and mentions playing state champ in high school. He also mentions seeing the Australia Open and the Masters tournaments. He reports consuming a lot of Easter candy recently, which may be contributing to his blood sugar levels. He has no fever reported.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals moderate lateral epicondylitis tenderness of the right elbow with pain on passive range of motion. Palpation reveals no joint effusion. Cardiovascular exam shows a grade 3/6 systolic ejection murmur. The right wrist exam reveals mild pain with resisted extension. The examination is otherwise unremarkable. Right elbow X-ray shows no acute fracture or bony abnormality.
medical_concepts: pain on passive range of motion

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The primary problem is acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow. The patient's diabetes is currently out of control, likely exacerbated by recent high-sugar intake. His asthma remains well-controlled.
medical_concepts: acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I will prescribe ultram 50mg once daily for pain relief. I will increase his metformin dosage to 1000mg twice daily. I will order a hemoglobin A1c and CBC blood test and a chem-12 blood test. I will also order a refill for his albuterol inhaler. I recommend rest of the right arm, cessation of tennis activity for 4-6 weeks, and icing the elbow 20 minutes three times daily. I will schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to re-evaluate.

medical_concepts: pain on passive range of motion
acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow
